A test script for a function that increments its argument by one.

# Purpose
This code defines a simple function named [`foo`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-007foo>) that takes a single argument `arg`, increments it by one, and returns the result. The functionality provided is narrow, focusing solely on incrementing a given number. The code also includes an assertion to test the [`foo`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-007foo>) function, verifying that when [`foo`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-007foo>) is called with the argument `1`, it returns `2`. This serves as a basic test to ensure the function behaves as expected.

### foo<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-007.foo}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-007.js#L15>)

Increments the input argument by one and returns the result.
- **Inputs**:
    - `arg`: The input argument to increment, expected to be a number.
- **Control Flow**:
    - Increments the value of `arg` by one using the prefix increment operator `++`.
    - Returns the incremented value.
- **Output**: The incremented value of the input argument.
